crypto/spiffe: use Ed25519 for SVID key generation (#145)

* crypto/spiffe: use Ed25519 for SVID key generation

Ed25519 is faster for signing/verifying, produces smaller keys and
signatures, and has safer deterministic signing compared to ECDSA P256.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* Update crypto/spiffe/spiffe.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Josh van Leeuwen <me@joshvanl.dev>

* crypto/test: add CRLSign key usage to root CA certificate

Standard CA certificates should include both CertSign and CRLSign
key usage to support certificate revocation list signing.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* Use correct non pointer for ed25519 keys in PEM encoding

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* crypto/spiffe: don't take pointer of ed25519.PrivateKey

ed25519.PrivateKey is a value type ([]byte), not a struct, so taking
its address creates *ed25519.PrivateKey which is not handled by
EncodePrivateKey's type switch, causing "unsupported key type
*ed25519.PrivateKey" errors at runtime. Use the value directly.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

commit.message:
fix: Infobox rechts ausgerichtet, Rahmen passt sich Schriftgröße an

Problem 1 – Position:
  panelX war fix auf ImageWidth * 0.04f (links) gesetzt.
  Neu: Panel wird rechtsbündig positioniert.
  Rechter Rand = ImageWidth * 0.04f (4 % Seitenabstand).
  panelX = ImageWidth - rightMargin - panelWidth

Problem 2 – Feste Breite:
  panelWidth war unveränderlich auf 340f gesetzt.
  Neu: g.MeasureString() misst für jede Zeile die tatsächliche
  Textbreite (Titel-Font bzw. Info-Font getrennt). Die maximale
  Breite bestimmt panelWidth = maxTextWidth + 2 * padding.
  Damit passt der Rahmen sich automatisch an FontSizeBase an.

Weitere Verbesserungen:
  - Einheitlicher innerer Abstand (padding = 16f) für alle Zeilen
  - Frühes Return wenn lines.Count == 0 (kein leerer Rahmen)
  - Entfernung der -10-Offsets (Inkonsistenz zwischen Fill und Draw)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add validation and unit tests AffineGrid (#27572)

### Description
<!-- Describe your changes. -->
This pull request improves the robustness and correctness of the
`AffineGrid` operator in ONNX Runtime by adding stricter input
validation and updating the implementation for better type safety. It
also introduces comprehensive unit tests to verify error handling for
invalid inputs.

### Input validation enhancements

* Added explicit checks in `affine_grid.cc` to ensure that the batch
dimension in `size` matches the batch dimension in `theta`, and that
`theta` has the correct shape for both 2D (`[N, 2, 3]`) and 3D (`[N, 3,
4]`) cases. This prevents silent errors due to shape mismatches.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L116-R126)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L131-R147)

### Type safety and code clarity improvements

* Updated grid generation functions to use the template type `T` for
Eigen vector creation, replacing hardcoded `float` types. This ensures
correct behavior for different data types and improves code clarity.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L31-R36)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L50-R60)
* Added clarifying comments to matrix and array initializations in
affine grid generator functions to make the code easier to understand
and maintain.

### Test coverage improvements

* Added a series of unit tests in `affine_grid_test.cc` to validate
input shape checks, including cases for incorrect tensor dimensions,
mismatched batch sizes, and invalid `size` lengths. These tests ensure
that the operator fails gracefully with informative error messages when
given invalid inputs.
* Ensured the required test utility header is included to support new
tests.

### Code cleanup

* Removed unused includes from `affine_grid.cc` to tidy up the codebase.

---------

the readTimeout refactor. The PS2000B needs up to ~100ms to process a
query and send a response; 20ms caused all reads to time out silently,

at every 0→1 and 1→0 transition. Applies to all four fader schedulers:

commit.message:
fix(config): remove Sentry wrapper from next.config.ts to restore standalone build

The static 'import { withSentryConfig } from @sentry/nextjs' at module
load time was crashing next.config.ts because serialize-javascript@7.0.3
(npm override from security fix) broke a transitive dep of @sentry/nextjs.

When next.config.ts fails to load, Next.js silently falls back to defaults
(no output: standalone) -> no server.js -> frontend_app crash loop -> 502.

Fix: remove the withSentryConfig wrapper entirely. Sentry still initialises
at runtime via sentry.client.config.ts / sentry.server.config.ts. The
withSentryConfig wrapper was only adding build-time source map upload
optimisations, and was never running in Docker builds anyway since
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN is not set during CI image builds.

The .next/standalone/server.js guard in Dockerfile will catch any regression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: DatabaseAdapter uses access token auth (not ODBC Auth attribute)

The ODBC Driver 18 in the container doesn't support the Authentication=
connection string attribute for AAD. Fixed to use the best-practice
approach: acquire token via DefaultAzureCredential, encode as
UTF-16-LE struct, pass via SQL_COPT_SS_ACCESS_TOKEN (attr 1256).

Changes:
- DatabaseAdapter accepts connection string OR server+database
- Strips any Authentication= clause from connection strings
- Token encoded as struct.pack('<I{len}s', len, bytes) per pyodbc spec
- Added create_sql_user.py script for granting MI database access
